stem: For the polynomial function g, the graph of y = g(x) in the xy-plane passes through the points (−3, 0), (2, 0), and (7, 0). Which of the following must be a factor of g(x)?
rationale: Choice B is correct. If (a, 0) lies on the graph of y = g(x), then x − a is a factor. Since (2, 0) is on the graph, x − 2 must be a factor. Choice A would correspond to (−7, 0). Choice C would correspond to (−2, 0). Choice D would correspond to (3, 0).
